npm_audit_summary: 61 packages installed in the temporary QA workspace; npm install reported 0 vulnerabilities.
performance_boundary: Frame timing is an uncapped local draw-loop measurement captured in the browser. It is not a vsync display FPS, load, soak, or production performance benchmark.

results.evidence: Real p5 package loaded from temporary QA node_modules. The artifact rendered nonblank and passed an internal canvas pixel check after 120 manual draw calls.
results.accepted_boundary: The real p5 runtime was installed in a temporary QA workspace, not added to the app dependency graph.

results.evidence: Real Three.js package loaded from temporary QA node_modules. The artifact created a scene, rendered nonblank, and passed a WebGL pixel check after 180 frame calls.
results.accepted_boundary: The real Three.js runtime was installed in a temporary QA workspace, not added to the app dependency graph.

results.evidence: The shader compiled, linked, drew, and passed a WebGL nonblank pixel check after 180 draw calls.
results.accepted_boundary: Validated in a generic local WebGL harness, not as a production-hosted preview surface.

results.evidence: Real hydra-synth package loaded from temporary QA node_modules. The artifact constructed a Hydra synth chain with detectAudio false, rendered nonblank, and passed a WebGL pixel check after 180 tick calls.
results.accepted_boundary: The real hydra-synth runtime was installed in a temporary QA workspace, not added to the app dependency graph. The harness disables audio detection and does not request microphone permissions.

claim_boundary: This is local browser QA evidence only. It is not a public deployment, not a display-FPS guarantee, and not evidence of DCC/MCP, autonomous immersive platform, or external execution. Hydra support is limited to the validated local hydra-synth browser artifact path.
